_comment: Curated NATIVE Microsoft Copilot Studio building blocks — the things that are NOT Power Platform connectors: authoring-canvas nodes, topic triggers, system topics, knowledge sources, and tools. These are hand-maintained from the public Microsoft Learn Copilot Studio docs (https://learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-copilot-studio/). build-catalog.js merges this file, tags every entry with products:["Copilot Studio"], and enriches tags. Connectors (from GitHub + Microsoft Learn) are tagged products:["Power Automate","Copilot Studio"] because they are usable in both products. Add a block by appending an object below; keep type to "action" or "trigger" so the existing Actions/Triggers filter still works.
connectorDescription: Microsoft Copilot Studio — native building blocks for AI agents (formerly Power Virtual Agents): topic triggers, authoring-canvas nodes, system topics, knowledge sources, and tools used to build and orchestrate agents.

blocks.name: Send a message (Message node)
blocks.description: Sends a message from the agent to the user. Can be simple text or richer content — images, videos, quick replies, and basic or Adaptive Cards. The foundational conversation node.

blocks.name: Ask a question (Question node)
blocks.description: Prompts the user for information and stores their response in a variable. Supports multiple-choice options, prebuilt or custom entities, and slot filling.

blocks.name: Ask with an Adaptive Card
blocks.description: Shows an interactive Adaptive Card with one or more response buttons or input fields, and saves the user's input to variables.

blocks.name: Add a condition (Condition node)
blocks.description: Branches the conversation based on a condition built from a variable, a logical operator, and a value (or a Power Fx formula).

blocks.name: Add a condition group
blocks.description: Authors and manages multiple conditions in a single node, with table or graph view, to reduce branching in topic flows.

blocks.name: Set a variable value
blocks.description: Assigns a value (literal, another variable, or a Power Fx formula) to a variable for use later in the conversation.

blocks.name: Parse value
blocks.description: Parses a value into a specific data type or entity and stores the result in a variable (variable-management node).

blocks.name: Clear variable values
blocks.description: Clears one or more variables, including the option to clear the conversation history used by the agent.

blocks.name: Redirect to another topic
blocks.description: Transfers the conversation to another topic (as a subtopic) and returns afterward, optionally passing input and output parameters.

blocks.name: Go to another step (Go to step)
blocks.description: Jumps to another node within the same topic, letting you loop or skip ahead in the conversation flow.

blocks.name: End the current topic
blocks.description: Ends the current topic and returns control to whatever called it (topic-management node).

blocks.name: End the conversation
blocks.description: Ends the entire conversation with the user. Optionally ends with a customer-satisfaction survey or a transfer to a human agent.

blocks.name: End with survey (CSAT)
blocks.description: Ends the conversation with a customer-satisfaction survey asking whether the user's question or issue was resolved; responses feed the CSAT analytics page.

blocks.name: Transfer to agent (human handoff)
blocks.description: Hands the conversation off to a live human agent (for example, Omnichannel for Customer Service), optionally with a private message for the representative.

blocks.name: Send an event or activity (Invoke activity)
blocks.description: Sends an event or other activity type (Typing, Invoke / Invoke response, Handoff) — used for custom client events and Microsoft Teams integration.

blocks.name: Send an HTTP request (HTTP node)
blocks.description: Calls an external REST endpoint from within a topic and stores the response in a variable. The Copilot Studio equivalent of an HTTP action.

blocks.name: Send generative answers (knowledge node)
blocks.description: Generates an AI answer grounded in configured knowledge sources for the user's question — used inside a topic or the Conversational boosting system topic.

blocks.name: Authentication (Sign in node)
blocks.description: Inserts a sign-in step so the agent can authenticate the user and access authentication variables (such as the access token) later in the topic.

blocks.name: Trigger: The agent chooses (generative)
blocks.description: For agents using generative orchestration. Fires when the agent decides the topic's name and description match the incoming user message.

blocks.name: Trigger: User says a phrase (trigger phrases)
blocks.description: For agents using classic orchestration. Fires when an incoming user message matches one of the topic's trigger phrases via the NLU model.

blocks.name: Trigger: A message is received
blocks.description: Fires when a message activity is received — i.e. whenever the user types or says something to the agent.

blocks.name: Trigger: A custom client event occurs
blocks.description: Fires when an event activity is received. Use the Event name property to filter for a specific custom event sent by the client.

blocks.name: Trigger: An activity occurs
blocks.description: Fires when an activity of any type is received. Optionally set the Activity type property to filter for a specific Bot Framework activity type.

blocks.name: Trigger: The conversation changes
blocks.description: Fires when a conversation-update activity is received — for example, when a user joins a conversation in Microsoft Teams.

blocks.name: Trigger: It's invoked
blocks.description: Fires when an invoke activity is received — most commonly from Microsoft Teams, such as when a user interacts with a message or search extension.

blocks.name: Trigger: It's redirected to
blocks.description: Fires when the topic is called explicitly by another topic via a Redirect node. Used for reusable subtopics.

blocks.name: System topic: Conversation Start
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that greets users and introduces the agent and its capabilities when the agent first engages in a conversation.

blocks.name: System topic: Conversational boosting
blocks.description: Built-in system topic containing a generative answers node; creates generative answers from your knowledge sources when no authored topic matches the query.

blocks.name: System topic: Fallback
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that runs when the agent can't match the user's message to any topic, informing the user and asking them to try again.

blocks.name: System topic: Escalate
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that informs users when they need to speak with a human; triggers when "talk to agent" is matched or the Escalate event is called.

blocks.name: System topic: End of Conversation
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that confirms the user's query was answered and closes the conversation; called by redirect from a custom topic.

blocks.name: System topic: Multiple Topics Matched (Did you mean)
blocks.description: Built-in "did you mean" disambiguation topic that prompts the user to choose their intended topic when a message closely matches several topics.

blocks.name: System topic: On Error
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that informs the user when a user error occurs, surfacing an error code, conversation ID, and timestamp for debugging.

blocks.name: System topic: Reset Conversation
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that clears variable values and forces the agent to use its latest published version.

blocks.name: System topic: Sign in
blocks.description: Built-in system topic that prompts the user to sign in when user authentication is enabled or when a node uses authentication variables.

blocks.name: Add knowledge: Public website
blocks.description: Adds a public website as a knowledge source so the agent can ground generative answers in its content.

blocks.name: Add knowledge: SharePoint
blocks.description: Adds a SharePoint site or documents as a knowledge source for generative answers (supported file formats only).

blocks.name: Add knowledge: File upload
blocks.description: Uploads files (PDF, Word, and more) from your computer as a knowledge source the agent can answer from.

blocks.name: Add knowledge: Dataverse
blocks.description: Adds Dataverse tables as a knowledge source so the agent can answer questions grounded in your business data.

blocks.name: Add knowledge: Azure AI Search
blocks.description: Connects an Azure AI Search index as a knowledge source for grounding generative answers in enterprise content.

blocks.name: Add knowledge: Copilot / Graph connectors
blocks.description: Grounds the agent in non-Microsoft enterprise data indexed into Microsoft Graph via Copilot connectors, respecting source-level permissions.

blocks.name: Knowledge sources & generative answers (overview)
blocks.description: Configure knowledge sources at the agent level so generative answers can find and present information from internal and external sources without authoring specific topics.

blocks.name: Tool: Prompt (AI Builder / GPT)
blocks.description: Adds a custom AI prompt as an agent tool or topic node — natural-language instructions to a generative model for tasks like summarizing, extracting, classifying, or drafting. Consumes Copilot Credits.

blocks.name: Tool: Agent flow / Power Automate flow
blocks.description: Calls a flow (agent flow or Power Automate cloud flow) as a tool from the agent, passing inputs and returning results into the conversation.

blocks.name: Tool: Connector action
blocks.description: Adds a Power Platform connector operation directly as an agent tool, so the agent can call any connector action (for example, send an email or create a row) as part of generative orchestration.

blocks.name: Tool: REST API / Custom connector
blocks.description: Extends the agent with a REST API or a custom connector as a tool, letting it call your own services.

blocks.name: Tool: MCP connector
blocks.description: Connects a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server as a tool, exposing its capabilities to the agent.

blocks.name: Agent flow: Run a prompt
blocks.description: Adds a Run a prompt node inside an agent flow (under AI capabilities) to call a prompt at any point in the automation.

blocks.name: Agent flow (automation)
blocks.description: Authors a flow natively in Copilot Studio (Power Automate-style designer) that an agent can trigger as a tool; supports prompts, connectors, conditions, and human review steps.
